title: Pda
weakest_section: 19:00-20:30 range with fewest/weakest jokes during rooftop reunion

jokes.text: Ryan and I have a huge announcement... Over the weekend, Ryan Bailey Howard and I got divorced.
jokes.setup: Kelly announces a 'huge announcement' with wedding-like excitement
jokes.punchline: It's actually a divorce announcement delivered with the same enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Kelly treating divorce with wedding-announcement enthusiasm. Strong misdirection with excellent structure and character integration.

jokes.text: Sweet. Free ring.
jokes.setup: Kelly announces her divorce
jokes.punchline: Creed immediately thinks about scavenging the ring
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - his morally questionable opportunism delivered with maximum economy. Could only come from his character.

jokes.text: Wait, I... Can you back up? What's the story?
jokes.setup: Kelly casually mentions divorce after marriage announcement
jokes.punchline: Andy's confused attempt to get basic timeline information
jokes.explanation: Solid straight-man reaction to the chaos, but fairly standard confusion beat.

jokes.text: We were making love constantly... Ryan was crying a lot.
jokes.setup: Kelly describes their romantic Poconos weekend
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions Ryan crying during their romantic time
jokes.explanation: Classic Kelly overshare with a disturbing detail delivered matter-of-factly. Good character integration.

jokes.text: That's not a relevant detail.
jokes.setup: Kelly mentions Ryan crying during their romantic weekend
jokes.punchline: Ryan's defensive dismissal of clearly relevant emotional detail
jokes.explanation: Ryan's defensive deflection works well as character moment, though structure is straightforward.

jokes.text: Um, like, a week ago. We got really wasted, and it just felt right.
jokes.setup: Andy asks when they got married
jokes.punchline: Reveals they drunkenly married a week ago on impulse
jokes.explanation: Standard Vegas wedding trope but fits Kelly's impulsive character. Fairly predictable setup.

jokes.text: And you didn't invite any of us? We are getting divorced, Andy!
jokes.setup: Andy focuses on not being invited to wedding
jokes.punchline: Kelly's exasperated reminder that divorce is the current issue
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Andy's misplaced priorities. Kelly's exasperation feels earned and natural.

jokes.text: No, with the messed up laws in this country, I don't want to be married until everyone can be married.
jokes.setup: Ryan needs to explain why he wanted divorce
jokes.punchline: Claims noble political stance while abandoning wife
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan - using progressive politics to justify selfish behavior. Good character integration with his pretentious nature.

jokes.text: You know what, Ryan? I talked to the other gay guys, and we're okay with it. We all agreed it's fine for you to get married.
jokes.setup: Ryan claims he won't marry until gay marriage is legal
jokes.punchline: Oscar deadpan gives Ryan 'permission' from the gay community
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Oscar moment - his dry, logical dismantling of Ryan's fake woke excuse. Perfectly delivered with great character voice.

jokes.text: No. No, Oscar. Not until everyone can.
jokes.setup: Oscar gives Ryan permission to marry
jokes.punchline: Ryan doubles down on his obviously fake noble stance
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Ryan's fake woke stance. Works as character beat but less impact than Oscar's setup.

jokes.text: This actually isn't amicable at all, and we actually do need people to take sides. Who's on my side? And who is on my side?
jokes.setup: Kelly and Ryan claimed divorce was amicable
jokes.punchline: Immediately devolve into demanding office choose sides
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and callback to earlier claim. Fits both characters' need for drama and attention.

jokes.text: Why does the sun rise in the morning? Why do magnets stick together? Because everybody says so. Everybody.
jokes.setup: Holly questions why they're destined to be together
jokes.punchline: Michael's completely illogical 'scientific' reasoning
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael pseudo-intellectual reasoning. Good character integration but structure is somewhat rambling.

jokes.text: Yes, I do. No, you don't. Yes, I do. No, you can have your own opinion, and I can have mine. I have my own opinion, and my opinion is to disagree with you.
jokes.setup: Michael and Holly disagreeing about having to agree
jokes.punchline: Michael's circular logic about opinions and disagreement
jokes.explanation: Rambling circular argument gets tedious. Poor economy and structure drag down what could be a character moment.

jokes.text: He's gonna be a lot of fun to drive around in a car with. Oh, you'll get through it, hon. Just make a game out of it. A funny Jim game.
jokes.setup: Pam observes Michael will be difficult to travel with
jokes.punchline: Holly's suggestion to make it a 'funny Jim game'
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Holly's understanding of office dynamics. Reference to Jim's pranks feels natural.

jokes.text: All right, what'd you do? Well, those things that you consider doodles, I consider art.
jokes.setup: Pam asks what Jim did to celebrate her talent
jokes.punchline: Jim's grand declaration about her doodles being art
jokes.explanation: Sweet Jim gesture with typical overstatement. Good character integration showing his supportive nature.

jokes.text: Three hints. One, when you're getting colder, you're really getting warmer. The fridge.
jokes.setup: Jim gives cryptic clue about location
jokes.punchline: Pam immediately solves the 'opposite' riddle
jokes.explanation: Standard riddle/treasure hunt setup. Competent but not particularly creative or character-specific.

jokes.text: Holly is ruining Michael's life. He thinks she's so special. And she's so not. Her personality is like a three, her sense of humor is a two, her ears are like a seven and a four.
jokes.setup: Erin criticizes Holly's impact on Michael
jokes.punchline: Rating Holly's asymmetrical ears separately
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Erin moment - the mathematical ratings escalating to absurd ear specificity. Perfect character voice and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Add it all up, and what do you get? 16. And he treats her like she's a perfect 40. It's nuts.
jokes.setup: Erin's mathematical rating system for Holly
jokes.punchline: Precise calculation and comparison to Michael's perceived rating
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the ratings system. Erin's literal-minded mathematical approach is perfectly in character and hilarious.

jokes.text: Well, I'm going the speed limit. Okay, fine. My feelings don't matter to you. What matters to you is your precious speed limit.
jokes.setup: Michael wants to drive faster, Jim follows speed limit
jokes.punchline: Michael's dramatic accusation about Jim prioritizing speed limits over feelings
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael dramatics over mundane situation. Good character integration showing his tendency to personalize everything.

jokes.text: Sex different after the baby, Jim?
jokes.setup: Michael is being ignored and wants attention
jokes.punchline: Asks extremely personal question about Jim's sex life
jokes.explanation: Pure Michael cringe - inappropriate personal question delivered casually. Great economy and character fit.

jokes.text: All right, let me turn on some music. I need to pee! No, you don't. Yeah, I do. My word against yours.
jokes.setup: Michael demands bathroom stop, Jim resists
jokes.punchline: Michael frames it as credibility contest
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of petty argument. Michael's logic is characteristically absurd but feels earned.

jokes.text: I'm upset, my bladder is full, there is no telling what I might do right now all over the inside of your precious little car.
jokes.setup: Michael demanding bathroom stop
jokes.punchline: Threatens to urinate in Jim's car with dramatic flair
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with callback to 'precious' phrasing. Michael's dramatic threat feels authentically childish.

jokes.text: A tiny thing. I locked her in the car. What? She's smiling. She's happy. Oh, my God.
jokes.setup: Helene mentions tiny fever issue
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals baby is locked in car, tries to downplay with 'she's happy'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from minor to major crisis. Helene's attempt to stay calm makes it funnier. Good structure and economy.

jokes.text: Excuse me, sir, there's a... There's a guy in the bathroom. He's coming out, but I have to go, 'cause it's an emergency. Will you just tell him to call the office?
jokes.setup: Michael desperately needs bathroom at gas station
jokes.punchline: Frantically explaining situation to stranger while rushing in
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy escalation. Works for Michael's character but not particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: But you're not holding a cup of coffee or anything, are you? Jim, what?
jokes.setup: Jim asks oddly specific question about coffee
jokes.punchline: Pam's confusion before he delivers bad news
jokes.explanation: Good protective husband moment - Jim's considerate setup for bad news. Well-structured misdirection.

jokes.text: I will put out an A.P.B., otherwise known as an 'Ask Pam Beesly.'
jokes.setup: Jim mentions putting out an APB for Michael
jokes.punchline: Pam's personalized acronym interpretation
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with good economy. Shows Pam's quick wit and self-awareness about her office role.

jokes.text: No, he ditched you, man. I saw him drive away. There's no way he ditched me. There's no way that happened. He's my...
jokes.setup: Michael insists Jim wouldn't abandon him
jokes.punchline: Can't finish sentence explaining relationship, revealing insecurity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's insecurity about relationships. The trailing off is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Actually, this is good. I am going to take this opportunity to go walk about.
jokes.setup: Michael is stranded at gas station
jokes.punchline: Frames abandonment as positive spiritual journey opportunity
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael defense mechanism - turning abandonment into adventure. Good character integration with his self-preservation instincts.

jokes.text: Problem solved, everyone. Space Orphan and Princess Nincompoop are off to rescue Michael.
jokes.setup: Dwight and Erin are going to find Michael
jokes.punchline: Dwight's elaborate nicknames for himself and Erin
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - grandiose self-regard with casual insult to Erin. Creative nicknames and excellent character voice. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: You drive. I got a car full of fox meat.
jokes.setup: Dwight offers to let Erin drive
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of car full of fox meat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight absurdity delivered matter-of-factly. Excellent economy and character integration. Could only come from Dwight.

jokes.text: Very funny, everyone. Who wrote captions under my doodle?
jokes.setup: Pam discovers her doodle has captions
jokes.punchline: Jim's 'art gallery' prank revealed
jokes.explanation: Standard prank reveal. Competent callback but not particularly creative or impactful.

jokes.text: 'I'm a suck, suck, suckity Sabre.' Booya!
jokes.setup: Reading caption contest entries
jokes.punchline: Kevin's crude company insult with celebration
jokes.explanation: Kevin's juvenile wordplay fits his character but is fairly basic. The 'Booya!' adds character touch.

jokes.text: 'I'm supposed to be wearing red gloves, but my color cartridge portal got jammed again.' Okay. No, no, no, no, no. Gloves. Keep it real.
jokes.setup: Andy reads overly technical caption
jokes.punchline: Darryl's street critique of the absurd printer reference
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Andy's verbose absurdity and Darryl's no-nonsense response. Character voices are distinct and authentic.

jokes.text: I've been reading the comics to my daughter since she was three years old. Not once have I used the real captions to Family Circus.
jokes.setup: Caption contest discussion
jokes.punchline: Darryl admits to always improving Family Circus for his daughter
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation about Family Circus being improved by any other caption. Great character moment showing Darryl's parenting and humor.

jokes.text: That crazy family is hilarious to her for one reason, me.
jokes.setup: Darryl explains improving Family Circus for daughter
jokes.punchline: Deadpan declaration of his comedic superiority
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff with excellent economy. Darryl's confident deadpan delivery is spot-on character work.

jokes.text: It is on like Genghis Khan wearing Sean John in Bhutan.
jokes.setup: Darryl declares caption contest competition
jokes.punchline: Elaborate rhyming escalation of 'it's on' phrase
jokes.explanation: Incredible wordplay escalation. Creative, unexpected, and perfectly delivered. Highly quotable and shows Darryl's verbal creativity.

jokes.text: Male, Caucasian, 40s, black hair, facial type, marsupial.
jokes.setup: Dwight giving description of Michael to gas station attendant
jokes.punchline: Describes Michael's face as 'marsupial'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight absurdity - treating police description seriously but adding bizarre marsupial reference. Great character voice.

jokes.text: He answers to Michael, Michael G. Scott, Michael J. Fox, Mr. Fox, the Incredible Mr. Fox...
jokes.setup: Dwight listing Michael's names
jokes.punchline: Escalating to movie character names Michael doesn't actually use
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight over-thoroughness but the escalation feels a bit forced. Fits character but not as sharp as his best moments.

jokes.text: Oh, really? You don't think he walked by that bakery just for the smell of it? She's right. He went that way.
jokes.setup: Dwight assumes Michael went back to office
jokes.punchline: Erin's insight about Michael being distracted by bakery smells
jokes.explanation: Great character insight - Erin understanding Michael's distractibility better than logical Dwight. Good misdirection setup.

jokes.text: Don't get a swelled head. You're no tracker. Let's ride.
jokes.setup: Erin correctly predicts Michael's direction
jokes.punchline: Dwight's dismissive comment while following her lead
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dwight's inability to give credit. Economy is strong with the abrupt 'Let's ride.'

jokes.text: Hey, you guys. Listen to me. Don't get hung up on just one girl, because there are a lot of other girls out there.
jokes.setup: Michael is alone and appears to be giving advice
jokes.punchline: Talking to pigeons like they're people with relationship problems
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael - projecting his own issues onto random situation. The visual of him counseling pigeons is inherently funny.

jokes.text: Hello. You guys are so beautiful. You're so colorful. I wish I could understand you. That's a metaphor, I guess.
jokes.setup: Michael talking to pigeons
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily declaring his statement a metaphor
jokes.explanation: Michael's pseudo-intellectual moment with pigeons. The self-aware 'metaphor' comment is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You are disgusting. You'll never find love.
jokes.setup: Michael having heartfelt conversation with pigeons
jokes.punchline: Stranger's harsh assessment interrupts his moment
jokes.explanation: Good interruption of Michael's moment. Harsh comment works as escalation though character integration is limited.

jokes.text: Pam, this isn't a shopping trip. No. This is a manhunt/rescue mission.
jokes.setup: Pam asks for supplies while they're out
jokes.punchline: Dwight's dramatic declaration of mission importance
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight dramatics about mission importance. Character voice is strong though the setup is fairly standard.

jokes.text: Pam, I'm obviously gonna get that stuff for you, so just shut up. God.
jokes.setup: Dwight refuses to do shopping during mission
jokes.punchline: Immediately agrees to do it while insulting Pam
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight contradiction - dramatic refusal followed by immediate capitulation with insult. Good structure and character work.

jokes.text: Number one, and this should be obvious, no captions that insult the company we work for. Irony is such a critical...
jokes.setup: Gabe sets rules for caption contest
jokes.punchline: Gets cut off mid-sentence about irony being critical
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gabe's pretentiousness being cut short. The irony of being interrupted talking about irony works well.

jokes.text: I think we can all agree they tend to alienate those who don't get the reference, making them feel like the other.
jokes.setup: Gabe banning pop culture references
jokes.punchline: Overly academic explanation for basic concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gabe - turning simple rule into sociology lecture. Great character integration though economy suffers from verbosity.

jokes.text: Sticky Quips are fun. They're safe. They're handy. I like to use Sticky Quips as regular Post-it notes when I'm in a fun mood. Not every day.
jokes.setup: Gabe promoting company product
jokes.punchline: Admission he only uses them when in a 'fun mood' and not daily
jokes.explanation: Great character moment revealing Gabe's careful management of his own fun. The 'not every day' qualifier is perfectly awkward.

jokes.text: Now, I don't have any money, so here is what I would like to do. I leave you here with my watch, and I come back later and I pay for the hot dog.
jokes.setup: Michael wants hot dog but has no money
jokes.punchline: Proposes elaborate pawn shop arrangement for hot dog
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael over-complication of simple problem. Character voice is strong but the concept is somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: I'm not a pawn shop. Well, I understand that, but this is a $45 watch. Wow. With that, I could buy half the menu.
jokes.setup: Vendor rejects pawn arrangement
jokes.punchline: Michael's watch is worth way more than a hot dog, vendor realizes
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Michael's watch is worth more than needed. Structure works well though concept is straightforward.

jokes.text: What do you do with the hot dogs that you don't sell? Throw 'em away. Well, okay, instead of throwing them all away later, why don't you just throw one away now into my mouth?
jokes.setup: Michael learns vendor throws away unsold hot dogs
jokes.punchline: Suggests 'throwing away' hot dog directly into his mouth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael logic - reframing eating as waste disposal. Creative, well-structured, and perfectly in character. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Okay. You've just lost my business.
jokes.setup: Vendor refuses free hot dog scheme
jokes.punchline: Michael threatens to withhold business he can't afford anyway
jokes.explanation: Good ironic threat from customer with no money. Standard Michael dignity-preservation move with good economy.

jokes.text: Hey. Hey, you were in there forever. There's too many brands. Where's Holly? She wandered off like an idiot.
jokes.setup: Dwight took long time in store
jokes.punchline: Blames choice paralysis, then casually insults Holly
jokes.explanation: Standard Dwight - practical excuse followed by casual insult. Competent character work but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: What are you doing? Just changing my cell phone plan. Okay. Here you go. I'll take my free stress ball too now. Sure thing. Here you go, Miss... Okay, Fanny Smellmore.
jokes.setup: Holly signing up for phone plan
jokes.punchline: Uses obviously fake name 'Fanny Smellmore'
jokes.explanation: Standard fake name joke. Juvenile humor that fits Holly's childlike nature but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Real original. What? You know what? Say hi to Orville Tootenbacher for me. Tootenbacher. Orville Tootenbacher, that's Michael's millionaire character that farts popcorn.
jokes.setup: Clerk sarcastically responds to fake name
jokes.punchline: Holly references Michael's absurd character, Dwight explains the popcorn farting
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Michael's weird characters. The 'farts popcorn' detail is absurd and memorable. Good character integration showing Holly's Michael knowledge.

jokes.text: I was gonna try to dine and dash, and that was stupid, and I realize I can't do that to you fine people.
jokes.setup: Michael caught trying to leave restaurant without paying
jokes.punchline: Confesses to attempted dine and dash while trying to sound noble
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael - confessing to crime while trying to seem moral. Good character integration but fairly predictable setup.

jokes.text: Well, the number three is not such a giant feast.
jokes.setup: Restaurant owner accuses Michael of dining too much
jokes.punchline: Michael's defensive minimization of his order
jokes.explanation: Standard Michael defensiveness. Character voice is right but the situation and response are fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Well, I think I can get through the door.
jokes.setup: Restaurant staff threatens to stop Michael from leaving
jokes.punchline: Michael's confident assessment of his door-navigation abilities
jokes.explanation: Good Michael confidence in face of confrontation. The matter-of-fact delivery about door navigation has good economy.

jokes.text: It's Michael!
jokes.setup: Dwight and Erin searching for Michael
jokes.punchline: Erin spots Michael just as they arrive at restaurant
jokes.explanation: Standard 'found him' moment. Convenient timing is predictable and not particularly character-driven.

jokes.text: You knew. What? No. Dwight asked if I wanted an egg roll. What... What are you implying? Uncanny.
jokes.setup: Dwight suspects Erin had supernatural knowledge
jokes.punchline: Erin reveals she just wanted egg rolls, Dwight still suspicious
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Dwight's paranoia and Erin's simple motivations. The 'uncanny' reaction is perfectly Dwight.

jokes.text: You crushed our spirits, Gabe. Congrats. You're a big man, huh? Takes a lot to destroy the creativity of a whole group of people.
jokes.setup: Caption contest fails due to Gabe's rules
jokes.punchline: Andy's dramatic condemnation of Gabe's rule-making
jokes.explanation: Standard Andy dramatic overreaction. Good callback to earlier rules but the condemnation is fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Click the 'X.' I'm clicking! In the box. I am clicking. Woman, you've had a computer for years! Phyllis! Too late!
jokes.setup: Stanley trying to help Phyllis close IM window
jokes.punchline: Phyllis's computer incompetence leads to 'Too late!' moment
jokes.explanation: Standard computer incompetence comedy. Fits characters but is fairly predictable and not particularly creative.

jokes.text: 'Darn it, Bob, I told you not to buy a Sabre brand lifeboat.'
jokes.setup: Reading caption for dogs on island picture
jokes.punchline: Kevin's caption about Sabre product failure
jokes.explanation: Decent caption connecting visual to company criticism. Good structure but not particularly character-specific for Kevin.

jokes.text: 'Wake up, Fred. The power cord on your Sabre printer shocked you into a coma, and you're dreaming you're a dog on a desert island.'
jokes.setup: Another caption for the dogs picture
jokes.punchline: Elaborate meta-explanation for why characters are dogs
jokes.explanation: Creative meta-explanation for the visual. Andy's overthinking nature comes through, though economy suffers from complexity.

jokes.text: How does the speaker know what the guy in the coma is dreaming?
jokes.setup: Andy's elaborate coma-dream caption
jokes.punchline: Gabe's literal-minded plot hole identification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gabe - missing the humor by focusing on logical inconsistencies. Good character integration and meta-commentary.

jokes.text: You don't have to sniff my rear end anymore, Bob. I'm the only one here.
jokes.setup: Gabe tries to write a caption
jokes.punchline: Creates uncomfortable dog behavior reference
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward Gabe attempt at humor. Character integration is strong but the joke itself is uncomfortable and not clever.

jokes.text: 'Is that a palm tree, or did Gabe get skinnier? Either way, let's pee on it.'
jokes.setup: Reading winning caption
jokes.punchline: Insults Gabe's thinness while suggesting urinating on him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed - combines visual observation with disturbing suggestion. Creative, well-structured, and could only come from Creed. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: It was easy once I decided I wanted the dog to piss on Gabe.
jokes.setup: Everyone praising Creed's winning caption
jokes.punchline: Creed explains his simple creative process
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Creed meta-commentary on his creative process. Perfect economy and character voice. The casual hostility is perfectly him.

jokes.text: Close your eyes. We need you to think. What is Michael seeing right now?
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to locate Michael
jokes.punchline: Treats Holly like psychic tracker
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight moment treating tracking like mystical ability. Character integration is strong but the concept is fairly standard.

jokes.text: Can you tell him that we miss him? Michael, we're coming for you!
jokes.setup: Dwight treating Holly like psychic
jokes.punchline: Erin plays along, shouting to Michael through Holly
jokes.explanation: Good Erin enthusiasm for absurd plan. Character integration shows her willingness to play along with anything.

jokes.text: I'm deep below the ocean's surface in a submarine. A torpedo's coming right at me. No. Damn it, that's just my own imagination.
jokes.setup: Erin trying to think like Michael
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by her own submarine fantasy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin moment showing her own imagination running wild instead of focusing. Great character integration with self-aware correction.

jokes.text: I just miss you so much. I missed you, too. Really? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Michael and Holly reunited on rooftop
jokes.punchline: Tender moment after Michael's absurd day of being lost
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that works as payoff to Michael's journey. Not comedic on its own but serves the story structure well.

jokes.text: 'I'm Gabe, and I'm a weirdo.'
jokes.setup: Reading more caption contest entries
jokes.punchline: Direct insult to Gabe in dog's voice
jokes.explanation: Straightforward Gabe insult. Good economy but lacks creativity. Standard workplace mockery without much craft.

jokes.text: 'Gabe's mom. Hmm, Gabe's mom? Wait, tall woman? Looks like Gabe? Yeah, I banged her.'
jokes.setup: Kevin reading caption about Gabe's mom
jokes.punchline: Escalates to claiming he had sex with her
jokes.explanation: Kevin's crude escalation fits his character but relies on juvenile humor. The buildup structure works but the payoff is predictable.
